report_name: Sandhills Medical Foundation Faces Class Action Probe Over Ransomware Attack Affecting 169,000 Patients

threat_actors.name: Inc Ransom
threat_actors.description: The 'Inc Ransom' group claimed responsibility for the ransomware attack on Sandhills Medical Foundation, listing the victim on its data leak website. The group operates a Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) model and employs double extortion tactics.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: Inc Ransom encrypted files on the Sandhills Medical Foundation's network to disrupt operations as part of their double extortion attack.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The Inc Ransom group exfiltrated the personal and protected health information of 169,017 patients between November 27-29, 2025, before publicly leaking it.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: A common technique for ransomware groups like Inc Ransom is to delete Volume Shadow Copies using tools like vssadmin.exe to prevent easy system recovery from backups.

malware.name: Inc Ransom
malware.description: A ransomware payload attributed to the Inc Ransom group, used to encrypt victim data and facilitate double extortion.

tools.name: vssadmin
tools.description: The vssadmin.exe utility is a tool commonly used by ransomware groups like Inc Ransom to delete Volume Shadow Copies, hindering system recovery efforts.

identities.name: Sandhills Medical Foundation
identities.description: A community health center in South Carolina that was the victim of a ransomware attack by the Inc Ransom group, resulting in a data breach affecting 169,017 patients.

infrastructure.name: Inc Ransom Data Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A data leak website operated by the Inc Ransom group where they listed Sandhills Medical Foundation and likely published the exfiltrated patient data.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8a1c9d-4e5f-4a6b-8c7d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://securityboulevard.com/2026/05/sandhills-medical-foundation-ransomware-breach-draws-class-action-investigation-nearly-a-year-later/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-04
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, though rare in automated scripts.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects commands used to delete Volume Shadow Copies, a technique commonly employed by ransomware like Inc Ransom to prevent system recovery.
